notes: Database is healthy. Query patterns are normal. The issue is in the application layer writing to it.

Queue depth is elevated — 57x normal baseline. Messages are processing but the volume of price stack updates triggered by the corrupted data is causing a backlog. No DLQ messages — writes are succeeding, just slow.
Service is running normally from an infrastructure perspective. The bug is in the application code, not the infrastructure.
Error rate alarm is in OK state — no application exceptions are being thrown. This is the detection gap: the bug produces wrong values silently.
Reconciliation job runs hourly and completed successfully. It validates field format and range but not business logic correctness — it does not catch the offset calculation error.
